perturbation.original_text: 1.2. Affiliate shall mean, (i) with respect to Qualigen, any corporation or other form of business organization, which directly or indirectly owns, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with Qualigen, and (ii), with respect to Sekisui, shall mean Sekisui Diagnostics (UK) Ltd., Sekisui Diagnostics PEI, Inc., SEKISUI MEDICAL CO., LTD., and Sekisui Diagnostics GmbH. An entity shall be regarded as being in control of another entity if the former entity has the direct or indirect power to vote more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting securities (or other ownership interest for a business organization other than a corporation) of that entity, or the direct or indirect ability to direct or cause the direction of the general management and policies of the other entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. Affiliate shall mean, (i) with respect to Qualigen, any corporation or other form of business organization, which directly or indirectly owns, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with Qualigen, and (ii), with respect to Sekisui, shall mean Sekisui Diagnostics (UK) Ltd., Sekisui Diagnostics PEI, Inc., SEKISUI MEDICAL CO., LTD., and Sekisui Diagnostics GmbH.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the sentence 'An entity shall be regarded as being in control of another entity if the former entity has the direct or indirect power to vote more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting securities (or other ownership interest for a business organization other than a corporation) of that entity, or the direct or indirect ability to direct or cause the direction of the general management and policies of the other entity' creates an In-Text contradiction because it removes a key criterion for determining 'control' of an affiliate, but the rest of the contract may still relies on the definition of affiliate in many sections of the document, where in those sections, they're reffering to the level of control an affiliate has on it's parent company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.22. Revenue Affiliate shall mean any entity of which Sekisui has the direct or indirect power to vote more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting securities (or other ownership interest for a business organization other than a corporation) of that entity, or the direct or indirect ability to direct or cause the direction of the general management and policies of that entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear definition of 'control' as it relates to affiliates. The changed text removes this definition. The contradicted text, Section 1.22, explicitly relies on the 'direct or indirect power to vote more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting securities...or the direct or indirect ability to direct or cause the direction of the general management and policies of that entity' to define 'Revenue Affiliate'. Without the definition in Section 1.2, it becomes unclear how to determine if an entity qualifies as a 'Revenue Affiliate' based on control, creating a direct conflict and therefore, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5. Technical Support. Qualigen shall provide to Sekisui and its customers commercially reasonable technical support (i) for the promotion, sale, after-sale service and support of Products sold in the Territory pursuant to this Agreement; (ii) in connection with any customer inquiries or complaints and (iii) in connection with interactions with the Regulatory Authorities. Qualigen shall be responsible for the management and costs of all such service. Qualigen shall be entitled to charge customers for, and to retain, commercially reasonable fees for service and support of out-of-warranty Instruments.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5. Technical Support. Qualigen shall provide to Sekisui and its customers commercially reasonable technical support (i) for the promotion, sale, after-sale service and support of Products sold in the Territory pursuant to this Agreement; (ii) in connection with any customer inquiries or complaints and (iii) in connection with interactions with the Regulatory Authorities. Qualigen shall be responsible for the management and costs of all such service.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Qualigen shall be entitled to charge customers for, and to retain, commercially reasonable fees for service and support of out-of-warranty Instruments', this creates an In-Text contradiction, because it's creating uncertainity as to who has the right to charge customers for support of out-of-warranty instruments. This section has been removed, making it unclaear who retains the proceeds of charges related to this service. Leaving this clause in place means, without this clause, Sekisui could interpret this section as allowing them to retain the proceeds, since qualigen no longer has the express right to retain this income.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5. Technical Support. Qualigen shall provide to Sekisui and its customers commercially reasonable technical support (i) for the promotion, sale, after-sale service and support of Products sold in the Territory pursuant to this Agreement; (ii) in connection with any customer inquiries or complaints and (iii) in connection with interactions with the Regulatory Authorities. Qualigen shall be responsible for the management and costs of all such service.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the clause 'Qualigen shall be entitled to charge customers for, and to retain, commercially reasonable fees for service and support of out-of-warranty Instruments' creates a direct contradiction with the repeated section. By removing this clause, the right to charge for and retain fees for out-of-warranty instruments becomes unclear, creating ambiguity where there previously was certainty, according to the explanation. This uncertainty could lead to disputes, therefore this is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.5. Adverse Events, Recalls and Field Corrections. Qualigen shall have responsibility to determine whether any Adverse Events, Recalls or Field Corrections information must be reported to the FDA (under United States law) or any other Regulatory Authorities and Qualigen shall have responsibility to prepare and submit notification of Adverse Events, Recalls and/or Field Corrections to respective Regulatory Authorities for the Products. Qualigen shall provide prompt notice to Sekisui of any Adverse Events, Recalls or Field Corrections, which notice shall in any event be delivered within no more than 3 business days from Qualigen learning of such occurrence.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.5. Adverse Events, Recalls and Field Corrections. Qualigen shall have responsibility to determine whether any Adverse Events, Recalls or Field Corrections information must be reported to the FDA (under United States law) or any other Regulatory Authorities and Qualigen shall have responsibility to prepare and submit notification of Adverse Events, Recalls and/or Field Corrections to respective Regulatory Authorities for the Products.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'Qualigen shall provide prompt notice to Sekisui of any Adverse Events, Recalls or Field Corrections, which notice shall in any event be delivered within no more than 3 business days from Qualigen learning of such occurrence' from Section 12.5 creates an In-Text Contradiction because it eliminates the explicit requirement for Qualigen to notify Sekisui of significant safety-related events. In the absence of this sentence, Sekisui may argue that they are not adequately informed to take necessary actions, which could include halting distribution or issuing their own warnings, if needed. Furthermore, the omission introduces ambiguity regarding Sekisui's responsibilities in managing recalls or field corrections, potentially leading to disputes over liability and operational coordination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.7. Product Recalls. In the event that (i) any Applicable Market governmental agency or authority issues a request or directive or orders that the Products be recalled or retrieved, (ii) an Applicable Market court of competent jurisdiction orders that the Products be recalled or retrieved, or (iii) Qualigen and Sekisui reasonably determine, after mutual consultation, that the Products should be recalled, corrected or retrieved in any particular country or countries, Qualigen and/or Sekisui shall conduct such activity and the parties shall take all appropriate corrective actions and shall execute the steps detailed in the recall strategy. Qualigen shall be responsible for the content of any communication to the customers regarding any Recall or Field Correction. In the event such action results from Sekisui's gross negligence or willful misconduct, Sekisui shall be responsible for the expenses thereof. Otherwise, Qualigen shall be responsible for the expenses of the action. Sekisui and Qualigen shall cooperate fully with one another in conducting any such action. Sekisui shall destroy units of Products lawfully recalled only upon Qualigen's (or any governmental authority's) written instruction to destroy such units of Products, and only then in accordance with Qualigen's procedures and instructions. Otherwise, Sekisui shall return the recalled units of Product to Qualigen in accordance with Qualigen's procedures and instructions after completion of the action.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence requiring Qualigen to notify Sekisui of Adverse Events, Recalls, or Field Corrections creates a contradiction with Section 12.7, which outlines the procedure for product recalls. Section 12.7 requires Qualigen and Sekisui to 'reasonably determine, after mutual consultation, that the Products should be recalled'. Without Qualigen being obligated to notify Sekisui of adverse events, Sekisui's ability to participate in this 'mutual consultation' is significantly impaired, creating a contradiction in the intended process for product recalls and corrective actions. The omission directly impacts Sekisui's ability to fulfill its obligations outlined in 12.7.
